title: `npm run lint` does not cover `lib/**`, so important runtime code can bypass the lint gate
reasoning: The package advertises `npm run lint` as the lint gate, but `next.config.ts` restricts ESLint to `app`, `components`, and `content`. Library code in `lib/**` contains production behavior, including Spotify token exchange and now-playing fetch logic, yet it is outside the configured lint scope. That means CI can report a clean lint run while missing issues in source files that ship to production.
recommendation: Remove the `eslint.dirs` restriction or add `lib` (and any other source roots that should be checked) so `npm run lint` covers all production code paths.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: This is a static-analysis coverage gap, not a runtime behavior that tests would naturally catch, and the provided excerpts show no separate repo-wide lint job that compensates for the narrowed scope.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a CI check that verifies `npm run lint` fails on a seeded lint violation inside `lib/`, or switch the lint script/config to traverse the full app source tree by default.
